entry.title: Can't add favorites in /IWFND/V4_ADMIN
entry.content:
I can't add favorites in /IWFND/V4_ADMIN, nothing happens after clicking "Add to Favorites".

I couldn't find any SAP note fixing this bug.

My system is ABAP 7.58 SP1, S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 ON-PREMISE.

How to fix it or any workaround?

For information, in the mean time, I have filed a case at SAP Support.

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Can't add favorites in /IWFND/V4_ADMIN
entry.content:
Unfortunately, only a code fix can solve it.

Here is a temporary fix which works for me, in the program /IWFND/R_V4_ADMIN_SERVICES, in ABAP 7.58 SP1:

The code fix is on the left side in the below screenshots; the ABAP code is also shared as text below the screenshots.

Code as text:

 

[...]

  METHOD handle_context_menu_sel.

    DATA:ls_node TYPE treev_node.            " <========== CHANGE
*    DATA:ls_node TYPE /iwfnd/s_v4_admin_tree.

    READ TABLE mt_tree_node INTO ls_node
      WITH KEY node_key = node_key.

[...]

FORM build_group_tree.

[...]

    ls_item-item_name =  gc_tree_service_group_column-column_service_group.
    ls_item-class = cl_gui_column_tree=>item_class_text.
    ls_item-info     = <ls_favorite>-value.              " <========== ADD
    APPEND ls_item TO mt_tree_item.
  ENDLOOP.

  " Node '/IWBEP/ALL' if exists
  READ TABLE mt_group ASSIGNING <ls_group>

[...]

FORM add_favorite USING is_node TYPE treev_node.     " <========== CHANGE
*FORM add_favorite USING is_node TYPE /iwfnd/s_v4_admin_tree.

  DATA: ls_favorite TYPE /iwfnd/s_v4_ui_favorite.

  DATA(ls_item) = mt_tree_item[ node_key  = is_node-node_key   " <==== ADD
                                item_name = gc_tree_service_group_column-column_service_group ].
  CASE ls_item-level_no.         " <========== CHANGE
*  CASE is_node-level_no.

    WHEN gcs_node_level-group_key OR gcs_node_level-iwbep_all_text.
      ls_favorite-id    = /iwfnd/cl_v4_ui_favorite=>gcs_favorite_id-group_key.
      IF ls_item-level_no = gcs_node_level-group_key. " <========== CHANGE
        ls_favorite-value = ls_item-info.             " <========== CHANGE
*      IF is_node-level_no = gcs_node_level-group_key.
*        ls_favorite-value = is_node-info.
      ELSE.
        ls_favorite-value = /iwfnd/if_v4_publishing_types=>co_group_iwbep_all.
      ENDIF.

    WHEN OTHERS.
      RETURN.

  ENDCASE.

  /iwfnd/cl_v4_ui_favorite=>add( ls_favorite ).

  PERFORM refresh.

ENDFORM.                    " add_favorite


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  remove_favorite
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM remove_favorite  USING is_node TYPE treev_node.   " <========== CHANGE
*FORM remove_favorite  USING is_node TYPE /iwfnd/s_v4_admin_tree.

  DATA: ls_favorite TYPE /iwfnd/s_v4_ui_favorite.

  DATA(ls_item) = mt_tree_item[ node_key  = is_node-node_key   " <==== ADD
                                item_name = gc_tree_service_group_column-column_service_group ].
  CASE ls_item-level_no.        " <========== CHANGE
*  CASE is_node-level_no.

    WHEN gcs_node_level-favorite_group.
      ls_favorite-id    = /iwfnd/cl_v4_ui_favorite=>gcs_favorite_id-group_key.
      ls_favorite-value = ls_item-info.     " <========== CHANGE
*      ls_favorite-value = is_node-info.

    WHEN gcs_node_level-favorite_serv.
      ls_favorite-id = /iwfnd/cl_v4_ui_favorite=>gcs_favorite_id-service_key.
      CONCATENATE mv_group_id
                  mv_system_alias
                  mv_repository_id
                  mv_service_id
                  mv_service_version
        INTO ls_favorite-value SEPARATED BY ' - '.

    WHEN OTHERS.
      RETURN.

  ENDCASE.

  /iwfnd/cl_v4_ui_favorite=>remove( ls_favorite ).

  DELETE mt_favorite
    WHERE id    = ls_favorite-id
      AND value = ls_favorite-value.
  IF mt_favorite IS INITIAL.
    CLEAR: mv_group_id, mv_repository_id, mv_service_id, mv_service_version.
  ENDIF.

  PERFORM refresh.

ENDFORM.                    " remove_favorite

[...]

 

For information, the favorites are stored in the table /IWFND/I_V4_MFAV.

entry.title: Re: How to set up a background job for transaction MR90 (...
entry.content: Hi Lakshmipathi, Thank you for the information, but that is was not enough. In the meantime, I found a solution. The RSNAST00 program must be used and executed only in the background. Additionally, the output type in NACE must be set to at least 1 (Send with periodically scheduled job) in the Dispatch time field. It may seem obvious, but it was quite tricky to finally resolve. Thank you everyone, and I’m closing the topic.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Kitting workflow to support CTO process
entry.content:
The CTO process used in my warehouse has a manual kitting and packaging operation, where the external manufacturing application generates a kit list. The operator looks up the inventory and sends an email to the storage locations to transfer materials to the packaging location. The parts are packed and returned to the location that initiated the request.  I am trying to find a way to implement a system driven process to manage this. I have three constraints that must be adhered to:
CTO process cannot be impacted. Solution must be additive only
Need to govern material flow referencing the order to maintain clarity of process. 
The packing team uses packing instructions in the current outbound parts shipping. The same operation needs to be followed to take advantage of efficiency and packing integrity.
I have reviewed use of Delivery documents without reference, STO between storage locations, sales orders with reservations, production orders.  All options seem to leave a critical gap. If anyone has attempted this with any success using best practices, could you share your approach?

We are using S/4 HANA with stockroom (LE-WM).

I do not want to deploy a partial solution. Any suggestions would be appreciated.

entry.content: This is not on Microsoft. This is clearly in your installation folder and will not be updated by Microsoft. Ignoring these is not an option. The vulnerable file is at this path. C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\Crystal Reports for .NET Framework 4.0\Common\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\libcurl.dll

Great article! 👍

Thank you for sharing this honest comparison. I completely agree with your conclusion — with a mature and well-featured CLI like @sap/datasphere-cli, building a full MCP server often adds unnecessary complexity and cost.

I took a similar lightweight approach and built a custom GitHub Copilot agent that works directly with the CLI through natural language. It focuses on simplicity, safety (especially for destructive actions), and easy adoption inside VS Code with no extra infrastructure. Your post reinforces my decision to keep it simple for now.

entry.title: IF YOU HAD TO RESTART YOUR SAP CAREER TODAY -- CAPM or UI5?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community👋

I am fresher who has recently started my journey in the SAP ecosystem, and I am currently facing an important career decision. I would really appreciate guidance from experienced
professionals in the community.


#The situation
Recently, I was asked to choose between working on :
1)SAP CAPM
2)SAP UI5/SAP FIORI

As someone at the beginning of my career , I want to make an informed decision that will help me ensure long-term career growth and stay aligned with SAP's future roadmap.

However, I am finding it difficult to decide which path would be better in the long run.

##SAP CAPM

From what I understand, SAP CAPM focus on:
1)Backend development
2)Cloud-based applications
3)OData services
4)Business logic
5)SAP BTP development

##SAP UI5/FIORI

SAP UI5 and FIORI appear to focus on:
1)Frontend development
2)UI/UX for enterprise applications
3)SAP Fiori apps
4)User experience design
5)SAP S?4HANA interfaces

##My Career Goals

As a fresher, I aim to:
1)Become a strong SAP developer
2)Stay relevant for future SAP trends
3)Explore opportunities in SAP BTP and cloud

 

###My questions to the community

I would love to hear from experienced SAP professionals:
1)Which path would you recommend for a fresher -- SAP CAPM or SAP UI5?
2)Which technology has better long term growth?
3)Which one offers more opportunities in the SAP ecosystem?
4)Is it better to start with one and later transition to the other?

entry.title: The Importance of Choosing the Right Data Type in Object Definitions
entry.content:
Recently, I encountered a critical issue while working on an object definition that reinforced a simple yet often overlooked concept — data type selection matters more than we think.

In this case, I was working on a custom MDF object Deduction Request (cust_Deduction_Request) in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central.

  • Field Name: cust_Amount
  • Data Type: String

This object was integrated via Integration Center to post data into One-Time Deduction (Pay Component Non-Recurring).

Everything seemed fine initially.

Here’s where things went wrong:

During testing, the integration worked fine because I entered only numeric values (without commas).

However, once it went live, users started entering amounts in a common format like 1,599 (with commas).

Due to this, the integration job started failing.

Error from Integration Centre:

Property value has invalid value. For input string: "1,599", required type is Edm.Double.
Additional One Time Payment & Deductions (EmpPayCompNonRecurring): Property value has invalid value.


What caused the issue:

  • The field was defined as String, so it accepted any format, including commas
  • Users entered values like 1,599
  • However, the target field expects a numeric value (Edm.Double)
  • Because of this mismatch, the integration job failed

Root cause:

On checking the object definition:

The field cust_Amount was defined as String, and there was no validation to restrict formatted numeric input.

We changed the data type from String to Number and re-tested the integration. After this update, the integration worked as expected.
While String fields can accept values in any format (with or without commas), this flexibility can cause issues during integration. Using a Number data type ensures that only valid numeric values are maintained.
This was a small but important correction aligning the data type with how the data is actually used.

Final Thought:

This was a small configuration decision, but it caused issues after go-live when real user input came into picture. It was a good reminder to think beyond testing scenarios and consider actual user behaviour.

entry.title: LGM Knowledge Snippet: Getting Started with FTL
entry.content:
If you've been working with SAP Logistics Management for LTL or Parcel shipping, you're familiar with the consignment-based workflow: Transportation Requests are grouped into consignments, carriers are determined, and goods are picked and packed before dispatch. But what about scenarios where you need an entire truck dedicated to a single shipment? That's where the Full Truck Load (FTL) shipping process comes in, and it just has been made available, Here are some additional infos for the process and it´s current limitations.

What Makes FTL Different?

In FTL shipping, a complete truck (or truck and trailer) is exclusively subcontracted for the shipper's cargo. Unlike LTL, where multiple shipments share carrier capacity and costs are based on weight or volume, FTL costs are driven by the truck itself - the time it's occupied and the route it travels, regardless of how fully it's loaded. We already support the Direct LTL process flow from the beginning (in there, the truck picks up cargo and delivers it to the customer without routing the pallets through the carriers netwrok/warehouses, but the truck is shared with other customers of the carrier) and the FTL process flow is quite similar as of now.

The FTL Process Flow

Here's how the FTL process works step by step:

  1. Logistical Demand arrives - A delivery notification from S/4HANA (or another source) triggers the creation of a Transportation Request
  2. Processing Variant is determined - Based on your Processing Variant configuration rules, the system identifies this shipment as FTL
  3. Freight Order is created automatically - This is the critical difference. Instead of creating a consignment first, the system immediately creates a Freight Order and assigns the Transportation Request to it
  4. Carrier is assigned - A carrier is assigned to the Freight Order, either automatically (if provided with the logistical demand) or manually through the Manage Freight Orders app
  5. Vehicle Type is maintained - To reflect the type of truck used for the freight order, the all new vehicle type has been introduced. So far, only a description and the weight capacity can be maintained in the Manage Vehicle Types app, but this might change in future.

     

  6. Carrier confirmation — The Freight Order is is communicated to the carrier, at the moment this is only supported with a manual process
  7. Warehouse processing is triggered - Only after the carrier confirms does the system release the Transportation Request for warehouse processing. This ensures the warehouse knows exactly which truck to load before they start
  8. Pickup/Delivery Document is created (technically, this happens in parallel to the warehouse processing trigger) - A PDD is created based on the confirmed Freight Order stop at the shipping point, coordinating the physical loading operation
  9. Warehouse execution completes — Goods are picked, packed, and staged for loading
  10. Pickup Execution: The standard processing of the Pickup/Delivery document including CheckIn, Loading and CheckOut is performed..

FTL and Direct LTL: Closer Than You Might Think

As teased before, if you're already familiar with the Direct LTL shipping process, the FTL flow will feel very familiar. In fact, Direct LTL and FTL follow nearly the same process pattern. Both share these key characteristics:

  • Early carrier determination — The carrier is known before warehouse processing begins. Important difference for now: FTL does not (yet) support tendering
  • Carrier confirmation gates warehouse work - In both processes, the system waits for the carrier to confirm before releasing goods for picking and packing
  • PDD is created from the subcontracting document — The Pickup/Delivery Document is generated automatically once the carrier confirms, rather than being matched from a pickup schedule
  • Early TR-to-PDD linking — Transportation Requests are connected to their PDD before warehouse processing starts (not after, as in Network LTL or Parcel)

The key difference between them is the document that orchestrates the process: Direct LTL uses a Consignment as its subcontracting document, while FTL uses a Freight Order. 

The Freight Order: Central Document for FTL

The Freight Order is the central planning and subcontracting document in the FTL process. Think of it as the equivalent of what a consignment is for LTL - but designed specifically for full truck loads. It contains:

  • Stops - Pickup and delivery locations with planned arrival times (at the moment, we support only 2 stops)
  • Partners -The assigned carrier, ship-to party, and other business partners
  • Assigned Transportation Requests - The goods that will be loaded onto this truck
  • Vehicle information - Vehicle type, utilization details, and capacity
  • Status tracking - Lifecycle status, carrier determination status, and carrier confirmation status

You can manage Freight Orders through the Manage Freight Orders app, which provides a list view of all Freight Orders with their key attributes and statuses, as well as a detailed object page for each Freight Order.

There is a new tile (with a slightly different truck icon, special thanks to our designers :-)):

 

 

Carrier Confirmation

One of the aspects of the FTL process is that carrier confirmation is a prerequisite for warehouse processing. The system will not release goods for picking and packing until the carrier has confirmed the Freight Order.

This design makes practical sense: in FTL, the entire truck is dedicated to your shipment. You want to be certain the truck is actually coming before you start staging goods at the dock. The confirmation flow works like this:

  1. Set the Freight Order to "Confirmation Pending" (send to carrier)
  2. Wait for the carrier to respond with "Confirmed" or "Rejected"
  3. Upon confirmation, the system automatically releases the associated Transportation Requests for warehouse processing

If the carrier rejects, the Freight Order is cancelled and a new freight order is created, where another carrier can be assigbed. This follows the pattern of the Direct LTL confirmation process.

Current Limitations

The FTL shipping process is actively evolving. Here are the current limitations you should be aware of:

  • Single pickup and single delivery only - Currently, each Freight Order supports one pickup stop and one delivery stop. Multi-stop scenarios (e.g., one truck picking up at multiple warehouses or delivering to multiple destinations) are not yet supported, though the data model is designed with this future capability in mind
  • No consolidation on Freight Order level - Each Transportation Request is assigned to its own Freight Order in a 1:1 relationship. There is no grouping engine that combines multiple TRs into a single Freight Order based on matching routes or time windows — even when it would make logistical sense to share a truck
  • No freight tendering integration - Unlike LTL consignments, Freight Orders cannot yet be sent through the freight tendering process to solicit competing quotes from multiple carriers. Carrier assignment is currently a direct, manual step
  • Manual subcontracting only - The subcontracting process for FTL is manual. While BN4L integration handles automated carrier communication for consignment-based processes, the Freight Order confirmation flow requires more manual interaction
  • No own-fleet support - The current FTL process is designed for subcontracted transportation only. Scenarios where you use your own vehicles to fulfill FTL shipments are not yet supported

 

References

  • Configure Shipping Processes (Processing Variants) - SAP Help Portal
  • FTL process configuration - SAP Help Portal
  • Direct LTL Shipping Process - SAP Help Portal
  • BN4L Integration Setup - SAP Help Portal
  • Processing Variants: The Idea Behind Configuration - SAP Community Blog

entry.title: What's New in SAP Subscription Billing (Q1 2026)
entry.content:
Welcome to our quarterly series highlighting the latest features in SAP Subscription Billing. This edition covers key enhancements released in the first quarter of 2026, bringing improvements in revenue analytics, data management, and pricing flexibility. Let's dive into what's new.

Recurring Revenue Analytics in Subscription Analytics Dashboard

Understanding your recurring revenue is essential for forecasting and strategic planning. The Subscription Analytics app now includes dedicated analytics for recurring revenue, giving you deeper insights into the predictable income generated from your active subscriptions. 

Two new pages are available: Recurring Revenue - Totals and Recurring Revenue - New and Churned. These pages provide comprehensive overviews and trend analysis of your recurring revenue within specific periods.

You can analyze key performance indicators including:

  • Current total recurring revenue
  • New recurring revenue
  • Revenue churn
  • Average recurring revenue per subscription

Recurring revenue analytics help you identify growth patterns, forecast future revenues based on existing recurring streams, and make informed decisions about budgeting, investment, and business scaling. 

The charts and key figures show trends and growth rates per period, and you can adjust the base period filter to view data by month, quarter, or year. The recurring revenue calculations normalize subscription amounts to daily values and then multiply by the number of days in your selected base period, ensuring accurate representation across different time frames.

Find out more at Subscription Analytics - Recurring Revenue.

 

 

 

 

 

Simplified Editing of Lookup Table Data

Managing your price conditions just got easier. The Manage Lookup Table Data app now allows you to edit individual rows directly, making quick adjustments to your price conditions without unnecessary complexity.

When you enter edit mode, you can modify any details. Need to adjust a price condition? Simply click on the price condition type to access the price condition builder, where you can fine-tune elements like price type, amount, and pricing unit.

For detailed information on managing data in lookup tables, see Lookup Tables.

 

 

 

 

Exclusion Groups for Refining Pricing Configuration

Pricing flexibility reaches a new level with exclusion groups in the Manage Pricing Schemes app. This feature allows you to compare multiple pricing steps and automatically select the most appropriate one based on specific criteria.

Four exclusion procedures are available to determine prices in different ways:

  • First Value in the Group: Uses only the first of the compared values
  • Last Value in the Group: Uses only the last of the compared values
  • Least Favorable Amount: Uses only the highest value or lowest discount among compared values
  • Most Favorable Amount: Uses only the lowest value or highest discount among compared values

Exclusion procedures with first/last values work well when comparing two pricing scheme steps. For example, you can use only two steps: one for the discounted price, and one for the normal price. By including both steps in an exclusion group and applying the first/last value procedure, you can conveniently control the pricing structure depending on the scenario. 

Procedures with least/most favorable amount are ideal for scenarios with more than two steps. For example, you can offer more favorable pricing for larger product volumes. In this case, you can create multiple price element specifications for the different volumes and set up the exclusion groups so that always the most favorable option is used in the subscription, depending on the scenario.

You can review how exclusion groups impact pricing in the Analyze Pricing Documents app.

That wraps up the summary of the most important features delivered in Q1 2026! We hope these enhancements empower your business operations. For further details, visit our What's New page on SAP Help Portal.

We encourage you to share your feedback, ask questions, or follow the SAP Subscription Billing tag to stay updated.

Until next quarter, keep innovating!
Katherine

More Information About SAP Subscription Billing

  • What’s New
  • SAP Help Portal Page
  • Feature Overview

entry.title: SAP S/4 Public How to assign a specific employee to an operation in a Maintenance order?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am currently working on a Maintenance Management implementation in S/4HANA Public Cloud and I’m looking for some guidance regarding Personnel Assignment at the Operation level and Capacity Leveling.

Current Scenario: We need to assign specific employees to Maintenance Order operations (e.g., assigning a specific task to "Sergio Bravo") and subsequently monitor their individual capacity/availability to execute the work.

What we have done so far:

  • We have configured and assigned employees using the app "Manage Teams and Responsibilities - For Work Center Asset Management" for a specific Work Center.

  • However, we are unable to see these assignments reflected in the standard maintenance apps or find a way to filter/view individual capacity based on these assignments.

Technical Questions:

  1. Is it possible to perform individual person-level assignment and capacity monitoring without Scope Item 43R (Resource Scheduling)?

  2. If Scope Item 43R is the only way to achieve this through the Maintenance Scheduling Board or Manage Work Center Capacity apps, are there any workarounds within the standard Core for tracking specific personnel availability?

  3. In the absence of 43R, what is the best practice for visibility of assigned technicians to specific operations in Public Cloud?

Any insights, documentation, or "Best Practices" regarding this specific requirement would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Sergio Bravo

entry.title: Extending Standard S/4 HANA OData API's
entry.content:
Introduction

Recently we build a solution to publish product data to a Kafka Event Broker, allowing consumer system to subscribe to different materials events based on the product type.  As part of the solution we wanted to make sure we keep a clean core principle, enable new standard enterprise events and API in the S/4 HANA backend, and evaluate the best alternatives to enrich and distribute the events considering the landscape available to the customer: S/4 2023 FP1, Integration Suite, Event Mesh, Advanced Event Mesh, and Kafka.

After delivering the initials solution, there was a clear gap for the business: "Where are our material master custom fields? "

I am not an ABAP developer, but I wanted to find out if the promise of easy extensibility is delivered. 
Spoiler: It will take you longer to read this blog, than execute the extension.

This blog show simplest approach to extend a standard OData the API. If you are curious about whole solution, comment on the blog and encourage me to blog more details on the other components too.

I will be using Product API for the example and screenshots, but the process should be applicable to most API too.

1. Enterprise Events and API

Default Enterprise Event in S/4 HANA are by default notification events. Their role is to notify of a change of state of an object, but the message will only include key parameter and not the context data for the object.
This also meant that for every standard event there is at-least one an associated API to extract the context data of the object.

Starting at the events is important, because this perspective will help you identify which API would the best for you to work with.

Familiarize yourself with Business Accelerator Hub: 
This link is for S/4 HANA On-premise: https://api.sap.com/products/SAPS4HANA/events/events

Here you can see the operations and schemas for events, if you are curious

 

Navigate to Documents, this will take you to the sap help documentation and is how you find the API related to the events

Make sure you select the correct version and feature pack as you landscape has.

 

2. OData V2 vs OData V4

Prefer ODATA V4 API when available. 

In the example above the related API is "Product Master (A2X)" is a Odata V2 implementation, but the system also has a OData V4 implementation.  OData V4 is always preferred as they are:

  • OData V4 implementation are more compliant to the OData OASIS specification
  • The service enablement process is simpler and will not require a custom interface object the the customer namespace. 
  • Has more extensibility option that OData V2 predecessors.

 for example: 

API

Product Master (A2X)

Product (Version 2)

Service Enablement TCODESERVICE_ENABLEMENT

SERVICE_ENABLEMENT

Maintenance TCODE

/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE - Activate and Maintain Services

 

/IWFND/V4_ADMIN - SAP Gateway Service Administration 

 

3. Extensibility Capability of APIs

In the SAP Help Documentation Find the Extensibility section for the API, and note of the object where you want to extend the API, in our case the custom field will be part of the main Product Entity.

This is important because you will need those objects when you  design the ABAP extensions

Technical name

Product Master (A2X)

Product (Version 2)

Extensibility 

5 Extensibility objects

 Note: A_Product

24 Extensibility objects

Note: A_Product_3

4. Eclipse and ABAP Developer Tools

The extensibility of standard API is by default possible using Eclipse and ABAP development tools. Maybe some ABAP gurus still can do a clean core extension in the ABAP workbench but I recommend to follow SAP recommendation to use ADT.

To download Eclipse and install the ABAP development tools follow this link: https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#abap

Assuming you have developer roles in the sandbox or developments system, and a developer key.

You should be able to add you ABAP system and to extend an API. 

 

You will need to create a new Data Definition  as under Core data Services. 
 

Use template  extendView, or extendViewEntity, if you are not sure start with extendViewEntity, you can always change the code template later or create a new one. 

5. Drilling down to the right extension point

In our example we want to extend the entity 

  • A_Product_3

With Field that already exist in table MARA.

extend view entity A_Product_3 with {
  mara.zmcat as ExtendedMaterialCategory,
  mara.zmcat_stat as ExtendedMaterialCategoryStatus
}

But you will see an error here, the table MARA cannot be found in entity A_Product_3, you will need to drill down and extend the additional entities and views on each layer.  

To start put your mouse over A_Product_3, open the context menu and select Navigate to  or Press F3

 

In the code for you will notices 3 things: 

  • The view entity is a  projection of R_ProductTP
  • Defined as with the alias "Product"
  • Still does not have MARA defined

 

Navigate to R_ProductTP

  • Is a root view selecting from interface view I_Product
  • still MARA is not defined, therefore no access to the custom fields.

Navigate to I_Product

  • I_Product is an interface this is the view selecting from mara
  • Jackpot 😀

6. Deploy and test.

In conclusion We will need to add 3 core data extensions:

  1. extendView : I_Product
  2. extendViewEntity: R_ProductTP
  3. extendViewEntity: A_Product_3
//ZI_PRODUCT_EXT

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZI_PRODUCT_EXT'
@EndUserText.label: 'Base Extension for I_Product'
extend view I_Product with ZI_PRODUCT_EXT
{
    mara.zmcat,
    mara.zmcat_stat
}
//ZR_PRODUCTTP_EXT

extend view entity R_ProductTP with {
  I_Product.zmcat,
  I_Product.zmcat_stat
}
//ZA_PRODUCT_3_EXT

extend view entity A_Product_3 with {
  Product.zmcat as ExtendedMaterialCategory,
  Product.zmcat_stat as ExtendedMaterialCategoryStatus
}

After successful deployment - test the API and you will see the extended field part of it.😎

Follow the same approach for extending other API with customer fields.
And the approach is similar if you need to extend with logic, associations or compositions.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2511 - MAIF Migration Program
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently in the process of migrating from SSAM 2405 to SSAM 2511 and are using the report /SMFND/CORE_MIGRATE_MAPP_CONFG to migrate the mobile application configuration.

We would appreciate your guidance on the following input parameters required by the program:

Source Application Pattern
Should this be the exact name of the existing (source) mobile application from SSAM 2405?
Or does it support wildcards/pattern-based selection (e.g., prefix-based)?
Target Application Pattern
Should this be the name of the new application in SSAM 2511?
Is it expected to already exist, or will the program create it during migration?
Source App Feature/Persona Prefix
What value should be provided here?
Is this meant to match existing feature/persona IDs (e.g., prefixes used in configuration tables), or is it used for transformation during migration?
Custom Objects Handling
Will the migration program also consider custom OMDOs and OData models created in the source application?
Are there any limitations or additional steps required to ensure custom configurations are migrated correctly?
Additionally, if there are any best practices or prerequisites (e.g., naming conventions, transport considerations, or known constraints) for running this migration program, please let us know.

I have reviewed the SAP documentation; however, some aspects are still unclear.

https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager/upgrading-sap-service-and-asset-manager/migrating-custom-configurations-to-newer-version-of-mobile-application?locale=en-US

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: Re: User Assistance Is Here To Help You!
entry.content:
Hi Kerisha,

To my knowledge, there's no such feature planned for Fiori apps (aka UI5 apps). 

However, if data elements are assigned to UI elements in Fiori apps and long texts are available, the long texts are displayed in the Help Panel. This feature has been available since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508 and SAP S/4HANA /SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025. This doesn't mean that the Performance Assistant is no longer available. You can always call it up by positioning the cursor on a specific field and using the <FN>+<F1> keyboard combination. In the Performance Assistant, you can then call up the technical details of the field.

Hope this helps.

Best regards,

Francesco

entry.title: Requirement of Custom Workflow of Integration of SD & PP Module In SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We received one requirement which need to map on SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud. The requirement is complex and in case of MTO which the SD & PP integration.

Please help and suggest the solution.

Requirement 

Required custom work flow to map MTO process with approval of 3 level the requirement is custom as no specific standard workflow available in SAP Public cloud after analysis sharing this with you 

Below are the discussed way-forward

1. Export End-to-End Process Flow

Step 1: Sales Order Creation

  • SAP S/4HANA Sales Order App
  • Mandatory Fields:
    • Export Order Type
    • Order Date
    • Customer Delivery Date (Mandatory)
  • Enhancement:
    • Add validation prevent save without delivery date

Step 2: Auto Notification to Stakeholders

Once order is saved:

Trigger Workflow / Notification to: Configuration basis.

  • Export Head
  • Procurement Manager
  • Logistics Manager
  • Production Manager
  • Export Billing Team
  • MD / COO

2. Department-wise Action Flow

Procurement (RM/PM Availability)

  • SAP Inbox Task
  • Action:
    • YES → RM/PM Available
    • NO → Enter Expected Availability Date

Production Planning

  • Triggered after Procurement update

Production Manager updates:

  • Production Start Date
  • Production Completion Date
  • FG Deposit Date

Logistics & Billing

After production completion:

  • Logistics:
    • Shipment Planning Date
    • Dispatch Date
  • Billing:
    • Invoice Date
    • Export Documentation Status

3. Status Tracking Structure

Create Status Flow Table



Stage

Status

Order Created

Completed

RM/PM Availability

Pending / Confirmed

Production

Planned / In Progress / Completed

Logistics

Planned / Dispatched

Billing

Completed

Final Status

Closed

4. Delay Alert Mechanism

Requirement:

If any department delays → Alert to ALL stakeholders

  • SAP Alert Notification Service

Logic:

  • Define SLA for each stage:
    • Procurement → 1 day
    • Production Planning → 2 days
    • Logistics → 1 day

If delay:

  • Trigger:
    • Email Alert
    • Fiori Notification
    • Escalation to MD / COO

Reporting:

  • Dashboard:
    • Order-wise status
    • Delay tracking
    • Department-wise performance

7. Workflow Logic (Simplified)

Order Created
   ↓
Notify All Stakeholders
   ↓
Procurement Update
   ↓
Production Planning Update
   ↓
Production Completion
   ↓
Logistics Planning
   ↓
Billing
   ↓
Order Closed

8. Automation Enhancements (Recommended)

  • Auto reminders every 6 / 12 / 24 hrs
  • Escalation Matrix:
    • Level 1 → Department Head
    • Level 2 → COO
    • Level 3 → MD

Final Outcome Expectation:

We will get:

  • Complete visibility of Export Orders
  • Department accountability
  • Automatic escalation system
  • Real-time tracking dashboard

Looking forward to hearing solution from you.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Looking for Standard SAP Solution for Master Data Creation Requests with Approval Workflow
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to ask for guidance on whether SAP provides a standard solution for handling master data creation/change requests. Specifically for Equipment, including an approval workflow and automated creation in the system.

Current Situation in Our Company

We have a Master Data Coordinator who is responsible for creating and updating master data in SAP. Our current process looks like this:

  1. A user wants to create or change an equipment.
  2. The user fills out a manual form/template explaining the required data and the reason for the request.
  3. The user submits this form into an approval workflow in SAP PLM.
  4. After all approvals are completed, the Master Data Coordinator manually:
    • Opens the form
    • Copies/pastes all values into SAP
    • Creates or updates the equipment

Challenges

  • Manual copy/paste introduces a high risk of human error.
  • The process is slow and depends heavily on one person.
  • We want to reduce manual work and improve data quality.

What We Want to Achieve

We are looking for a standard SAP process or Fiori app that would allow:

  1. A user submits a request to create an equipment directly in SAP (via Fiori or another standard interface).
  2. The request to go through an approval workflow.
  3. After approval, the system should automatically create the equipment in SAP without manual intervention.

Essentially, we want to replace the manual form + manual data entry with a more integrated, automated, and error‑free process.

My Questions to the Community

  • Does SAP offer a standard Fiori app or standard workflow for equipment creation/change requests?
  • Is there a standard SAP component (e.g., PLM, EAM-specific apps) that supports:
    • Request submission
    • Approval workflow
    • Automatic creation of equipment after approval
  • If yes, what would be the recommended approach?

Why This Matters

We want to avoid building a large custom solution if SAP already provides something standard. Our goal is to:

  • Improve data quality
  • Reduce manual work
  • Minimize errors
  • Streamline the approval process

Any insights, recommendations, or experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: AGP’s Next Chapter: Dual SAP Certification Fuels Cloud Growth Ambitions
entry.content:
AGP, a specialized SAP service provider based in Chile, has reached an important milestone in its growth journey: achieving its first-ever certifications for SAP SuccessFactors HCM Operations and SAP BTP Operations. But for AGP, this achievement goes beyond formal recognition. It reflects a deliberate effort to strengthen quality, standardize processes, and further mature as an organization. In this interview, AGP shares what motivated them to pursue certification at this stage, how the audit process catalyzed internal improvement, and why they see certification as a foundation for long-term excellence rather than a one-time validation. The conversation also explores AGP’s expanding role in cloud transformation - building capabilities in integration, extensibility, and automation - and outlines its vision to become a leading SAP partner in the region.

Could you start by telling us about your company – what services do you offer, and which industries or customer types do you primarily serve?

AGP is a specialized SAP service provider focused on implementing, supporting, and optimizing solutions such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP BTP. We primarily work with large-scale organizations across industries such as healthcare, mining, transportation, and services, where operational quality and business continuity are critical. Our main differentiator is our team: highly committed professionals with international experience who work closely with our clients, understand their businesses, and act as strategic partners.

AGP team

 

You were recently certified for the first time for SAP SuccessFactors HCM operations and SAP BTP operations. What motivated you to pursue these certifications at this point in your journey?

The certification represents a natural step in our evolution. We have been strengthening our SAP operations with a strong focus on quality, processes, and specialization, and these certifications formally validate that path. Our main motivation was to reassure our clients that we operate under SAP-recognized standards, reinforcing our value proposition based on trust, excellence, and delivery capability.

 

Looking back, how did you experience the audit process? And did it give you any new perspectives – whether on your internal governance, quality standards, or ways of working?

The process was very enriching. It allowed us to confirm that our practices are aligned with international standards, while also identifying opportunities for improvement in governance, documentation, and standardization. More than an audit, we experienced it as a step forward in organizational maturity, reinforcing our commitment to continuous improvement.

 

SAP is strongly focused on the cloud. How are you already supporting customers in their migration, and where do you plan to expand?

We are already supporting our customers in their transition to the cloud, especially SAP SuccessFactors and SAP BTP solutions. We participate in both implementation and AMS services, ensuring the solutions' continuity, evolution, and adoption. Our expansion focus is on strengthening capabilities in integration, extensibility, and automation on SAP BTP, as well as enabling more agile and scalable cloud operating models.

How do you plan to differentiate yourself and leverage the certification – both in the market and in your communication with customers?

Our differentiation goes beyond the certification itself - it lies in how we work. The certification supports a model based on close client relationships, high-level teams, and a strong service culture. We will leverage it to reinforce market trust, demonstrating that we combine SAP standards with a human, flexible, and results-oriented approach.

Where do you see the biggest growth opportunities, and how do you plan to scale your team to meet demand?

We see significant opportunities in cloud adoption, especially in talent management solutions and innovation platforms like SAP BTP. To scale, we are strongly investing in local talent development, training and certifying new consultants, while strengthening our team with senior profiles who bring experience and culture. We believe sustainable growth is achieved by building strong teams, not just increasing capacity.

What are your strategic plans to deepen your partnership with SAP in the coming years?

Our goal is to position ourselves as a leading partner in the region. We aim to continue expanding our certifications, strengthening our cloud capabilities, and co-innovating with SAP to deliver real value to our customers. Our relationship with SAP is strategic, and we see it as a long-term partnership built on trust, quality, and shared growth.

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.

entry.title: Integrating India GST Reporting, E-Invoicing, and E-Waybills with DRC Cloud Edition
entry.content:
• Architecture
  • Scope Items Required in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Step 1: DRC Cloud Edition Setup
  • 1.1 Initial Setup (One-time)
  • 1.1.1 Create a Subaccount
  • 1.1.2 Configure Entitlements
  • 1.1.3 Subscribe to the Application
  • 1.1.4 Assign Role Collections
  • 1.1.5 Create a Service Instance
  • 1.2 Activate India Processes
  • 1.3 Create Service Binding
  • Step 2: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Configuration
  • 2.1 Configure Business Places and GSP Settings (Mandatory — per Business Place)
  • 2.2 Export SSL Client Certificate
  • 2.3 Create Communication System in S/4HANA
  • 2.4 Create Communication Arrangement in S/4HANA
  • Step 2B: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Configuration
  • 2B.1 Configure Business Places and GSP Settings
  • 2B.2 Authentication: Choose Your Approach
  • Option A: X.509 (User Provided SSL Client Certificate)
  • Option B: OAuth 2.0
  • 3.1 Managing Destination Certificates
  • 3.2 Set Up Connectivity (DRC → S/4HANA)
  • 3.3 Map Business Systems
  • 3.4 Upload SSL Client Certificate (from S/4HANA)
  • 3.5 Configure GSP Communication Settings
  • GST Return (India)
  • E-Invoices (India)
  • E-Waybills (India)
  • Obtaining GSTINs for Sandbox Testing
  • Common Pitfalls
  • Pre-Go-Live Checklist
  • DRC Cloud Edition Setup (on SAP BTP)
  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • DRC Cloud Edition
  • Testing
  • References

 

This blog is a guide to the integration between your S/4HANA system and DRC Cloud Edition (a service running on SAP BTP) for India GST compliance. Whether you are running S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition or S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, the overall integration pattern is the same — S/4HANA connects to DRC Cloud Edition, which routes submissions to GSTN through a GSP. What differs is how the S/4HANA side is configured. This blog covers both, alongside the DRC setup steps that are common to both editions, and how to get started with sandbox GSTINs for testing.

Architecture

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition   S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
         │                                  │
         │  (X.509 or OAuth 2.0)            │  (X.509 or OAuth 2.0)
         └──────────────┬───────────────────┘
                        │
                        ▼
          DRC Cloud Edition (hosted on SAP BTP)
                        │
                        │  (GSP API calls)
                        ▼
              GSP (e.g., Vayana, Excellon)
                        │
                        ▼
                   GSTN Portal

Component Role

S/4HANA Cloud Public EditionSource of GST transaction data; connects to DRC via Communication Arrangement (SAP_COM_0854)
S/4HANA Cloud Private EditionSource of GST transaction data; connects to DRC via SOA Management logical ports (X.509) or OAuth 2.0 client + RFC connection
DRC Cloud Edition (on SAP BTP)SAP BTP-hosted service that orchestrates GST report submission; manages GSP communication, service bindings, and connectivity back to S/4HANA
GST Suvidha Provider (GSP)GSTN-certified intermediary that relays API calls between DRC Cloud Edition and GSTN
GSTNGovernment portal that stores and validates GST returns

Scope Items Required in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Note: The scope items below (5XU and 78L) apply to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition only and are activated via SAP Central Business Configuration. For S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, the corresponding India capabilities are activated through SAP Best Practices scope items (for example, INV12 for 2025). Refer to SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (India) for scope item details and activation guidance specific to your release.

Activate both scope items before any configuration work begins:

Scope Item Description

5XUDocument and Reporting Compliance — enables India GST statutory reporting (GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, GSTR-6, GSTR-9, ITC04, etc.) via Run Statutory Reports
78LSAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition Integration — activates the communication framework between S/4HANA Cloud and DRC Cloud Edition

Step 1: DRC Cloud Edition Setup

DRC Cloud Edition runs on SAP BTP. This step covers provisioning the service and configuring it for India GST — the service binding URL generated here is needed in S/4HANA.

1.1 Initial Setup (One-time)

Option A – Booster (Recommended): (SAP Help: Initial Setup)

  1. In BTP Cockpit, go to your global account → Boosters.
  2. Search for "Set Up an Account for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition" and run it.
  3. The booster automatically: creates the subaccount, configures entitlements, subscribes to the application, creates the service instance, and assigns role collections.

Option B – Manual Setup:

1.1.1 Create a Subaccount

Create a subaccount in a region that supports India GST processes. Refer to Subaccount Regions for Processes in the Cloud Edition in SAP Help to verify region availability.

1.1.2 Configure Entitlements

Assign the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition service entitlement to your subaccount and add the appropriate service plans. Two plans are relevant for S/4HANA integration:

Service Plan Purpose

standardProduction use — DRC connects to the production environment of GSTN / NIC
testTesting use — DRC connects to the sandbox/test environment of GSTN / NIC
Important: Use separate subaccounts for your test and production environments, each with the corresponding service plan. Using the test plan ensures all API calls go to the GSTN/NIC Sandbox and never reach the production system. See Cloud Edition Service Plans for a full overview.

For the service instance itself, one plan is available regardless of subscription: Default.

1.1.3 Subscribe to the Application

In your subaccount → Services → Service Marketplace → find SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition → Subscribe. Select the plan that matches the purpose of your subaccount (standard for production, test for testing).

1.1.4 Assign Role Collections

Assign the predefined DRC role collections to administrators and report operators.

1.1.5 Create a Service Instance

In Services → Instances and Subscriptions, create a service instance for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition. This instance is the connection endpoint used by S/4HANA.

Note: If you have already completed initial setup for any other DRC process/country, you do not need to repeat it — the same service instance can serve multiple processes, provided they are in the same subaccount region.

1.2 Activate India Processes

  1. Open the DRC Cloud Edition Fiori Launchpad from BTP.
  2. Navigate to Manage Processes → Activating Processes.
  3. Activate the relevant India processes for your subaccount:

Process Covers

GST Return (India)All India GST returns: GSTR-1, GSTR-1A, GSTR-3B, GSTR-6, GSTR-9, ITC04 — no need to activate individual return types separately
E-Invoices (India)Electronic invoice registration with NIC (National Informatics Centre) via GSP
E-Waybills (India)Electronic waybill generation and submission with NIC via GSP
Activate only the processes relevant to your deployment. Each is independent — you can activate GST Return (India) without activating E-Invoices (India), or any combination that fits your business needs.

1.3 Create Service Binding

  1. In BTP Cockpit → your subaccount → Services → Instances and Subscriptions.
  2. Select the DRC service instance.
  3. In the Service Bindings panel, click Create:

Field Value

Binding NameA descriptive name (e.g., india-gst-s4hana-binding)
ProcessOther Processes
Authentication TypeChoose one of the two supported options below

Authentication Type options:

  • X.509 Certificate (SSL): Select User Provided X.509 Certificate. The S/4HANA system presents its SSL client certificate to authenticate with DRC. Supported for both Public and Private Edition. For Private Edition, this certificate is also used when configuring logical ports in SOA Management. See Creating Service Bindings Using User Provided SSL Client Certificate.
  • OAuth 2.0: Select oAuth2. DRC generates client credentials; you configure an OAuth 2.0 client in S/4HANA using those credentials. Supported for both Public and Private Edition. See Creating Service Bindings to Use OAuth 2.0 Client Authentication.
  1. After creation, open the binding and copy the URL — this is the DRC endpoint URL you will configure in S/4HANA's Communication System.

Step 2: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Configuration

2.1 Configure Business Places and GSP Settings (Mandatory — per Business Place)

Step 1 — Assign GSTIN to Business Place:

Use SSCUI 101326 (Define Business Places for Goods and Services Tax for India) to assign the GSTIN registered for each Business Place and Company Code.

Step 2 — Maintain GSP Name:

In Configuration Environment, search for "Maintain Service Provider for eInvoice" and enter the GSP Name (e.g., vayana, excellon) for each Business Place — this is the certified intermediary DRC will route API calls through.

Note: In the NIC portal, you register a User ID against each GSTIN. This GSTIN + GSP pairing drives how the system routes all API calls via DRC Cloud Edition. If you are also using eWay Bill, maintain the same configuration in Maintain Service Provider for eWay Bill.

2.2 Export SSL Client Certificate

  1. In the S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad, open Communication Systems.
  2. Locate (or create) the system entry and export the SSL client certificate.
  3. This certificate will be uploaded into DRC Cloud Edition to establish mutual TLS trust.

2.3 Create Communication System in S/4HANA

  1. Open the Communication Systems app.
  2. Create a new Communication System:
  • Host Name: the DRC service binding URL copied from Step 1.3.
  • Upload or reference the DRC-side certificate for trust.

2.4 Create Communication Arrangement in S/4HANA

  1. Open the Communication Arrangements app.
  2. Create a new arrangement for communication scenario SAP_COM_0854 (Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition Integration — requires scope item 78L). See Creating Communication Arrangements.
  3. Select the Communication System from Step 2.3.
  4. Fill the Integration Scenario field — this maps the generated RFC destination to the correct DRC process.
  5. Save. The system generates an inbound service URL — note this down for Step 3.2.
Critical: Only one Communication Arrangement is permitted per communication scenario. If one already exists, review it before creating a new one.

Step 2B: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Configuration

Note: Skip this section if you are using S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition — see Step 2 above. Private Edition does not use Communication Systems or Communication Arrangements. Instead, it connects to DRC via SOA Management logical ports (for X.509) or an OAuth 2.0 client with an RFC connection.

See Integrating All Other Processes and Countries/Regions with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for the full guide.

2B.1 Configure Business Places and GSP Settings

Same as Step 2.1 for Public Edition:

  • Use SSCUI 101326 (Define Business Places for Goods and Services Tax for India) to assign the GSTIN to each Business Place and Company Code.
  • In Configuration Environment, search for "Maintain Service Provider for eInvoice" and enter the GSP Name (e.g., vayana, excellon) per Business Place.

2B.2 Authentication: Choose Your Approach

Private Edition supports two authentication options for the connection to DRC Cloud Edition. Choose one and follow the corresponding steps.

Option A: X.509 (User Provided SSL Client Certificate)

This is the same certificate-based approach available for Public Edition, adapted for Private Edition using SOA Management. See User Provided SSL Client Certificate Authentication.

Step 1 — Import the SSL Client Certificate:

  1. In your S/4HANA system, open transaction STRUST (SSL Client PSE).
  2. Import or generate the SSL client certificate that will identify your system to DRC.
  3. Export the certificate (public key) — you will upload it into the service binding on BTP.

Step 2 — Create Service Binding with User Provided X.509 (on BTP):

  1. In BTP Cockpit, create the service binding for the DRC instance (as described in Step 1.3).
  2. For Authentication Type, select User Provided X.509 Certificate.
  3. Upload the certificate exported in Step 1.
  4. Copy the URL generated by the service binding.

Step 3 — Configure Logical Port in SOA Management:

  1. In your S/4HANA system, open transaction SOAMANAGER (SOA Management).
  2. On the Service Administration tab, choose Web Service Configuration.
  3. Search for the relevant proxy using CO_EDO or CO_DCS.
  4. For each proxy, create a logical port (Create → Manual Configuration😞
  • Consumer Security: Select X.509 SSL Client Certificate and enter the certificate from STRUST.
  • HTTP Settings: Set Protocol to HTTPS, Host to the DRC service binding URL, Port to 443, and Path to the appropriate test or production path.
  • SOAP Protocol: Choose Suppress ID Transfer.
  1. After saving, choose Ping Web Service to verify the connection.
Note: If the authentication option is not selectable in SOA Management, refer to SAP Notes 2368112 and 510007.

Option B: OAuth 2.0

In this approach, DRC issues OAuth client credentials that your S/4HANA system uses to authenticate every call. This requires an RFC connection (SM59) rather than SOA Management logical ports.

Step 1 — Configure OAuth 2.0 Client Profile:

Before configuring the OAuth 2.0 client, create the required client profile in your S/4HANA system. The profile is used to group and identify the OAuth 2.0 configuration for DRC. Refer to the DRC documentation for the exact profile name to use.

Step 2 — Configure OAuth 2.0 Client:

Use the credentials from the service binding created in Step 1.3 (Authentication Type: oAuth2

  1. In your S/4HANA system, open transaction OA2C_CONFIG (OAuth 2.0 Clients) and create a new entry.
  2. Select the OAuth 2.0 Client Profile created in Step 1.
  3. Fill in the following fields using values from the service binding details in BTP:

Field Source

OAuth 2.0 Client IDclientid from service binding
Client Secretclientsecret from service binding
Token Endpoint URLToken URL from service binding

See Configuring the OAuth 2.0 Client.

Step 3 — Configure Secure Connection:

  1. Download the server root certificate from the DRC service binding in BTP.
  2. In your S/4HANA system, open transaction STRUST (SSL Client PSE) and import the certificate into the appropriate SSL Client PSE.
  3. This establishes TLS trust from your S/4HANA system toward DRC Cloud Edition.

See Configuring a Secure Connection.

Step 4 — Create RFC Connection:

  1. In your S/4HANA system, open transaction SM59 (Configuration of RFC Connections).
  2. Create a new HTTP connection to an external server with the following settings:

Field Value

Connection TypeG (HTTP Connection to External Server)
Target HostDRC service binding URL from Step 1.3
Logon ProcedureOAuth 2.0 — select the client configured in Step 2
  1. Activate the RFC connection. The system generates an inbound service URL — note this down for Step 3.2.

See Managing RFC Connections for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.

3.1 Managing Destination Certificates

To establish the return path from DRC back to S/4HANA, you first manage destination certificates in the DRC setup. This step is performed in SAP BTP as part of the overall DRC → S/4HANA connectivity setup.

  1. In the DRC setup (under Establishing Connection from Cloud Edition to Business System), navigate to Managing Destination Certificates.
  2. Generate or upload a certificate that authenticates the DRC Cloud Edition to your S/4HANA system.
Reference: Establishing Connection from Cloud Edition to Business System

3.2 Set Up Connectivity (DRC → S/4HANA)

  1. In the DRC setup, go to Setting Up Connectivity.
  2. Configure the connection from DRC Cloud Edition to your S/4HANA system using the inbound service URL:
  • Public Edition: the URL generated when you saved the Communication Arrangement in Step 2.4.
  • Private Edition (OAuth 2.0): the URL from the RFC connection activated in Step 2B (Option B, Step 4).
  • Private Edition (X.509): the endpoint exposed via the SOA Management logical port configured in Step 2B (Option A, Step 3).
  1. This allows DRC to push filing status updates and authority responses back to S/4HANA.

3.3 Map Business Systems

  1. In DRC, open the Map Business Systems app.
  2. Obtain your Own SAP Cloud System ID from S/4HANA (via Getting Your Own SAP Cloud System ID).
  3. Map your S/4HANA system to the destination configured in Step 3.2 so DRC knows how to route data between external communication parties and your business system.

3.4 Upload SSL Client Certificate (from S/4HANA)

Applies to X.509 authentication only (both Public Edition and Private Edition using Option A). If you are using OAuth 2.0 authentication, DRC authenticates via OAuth token, and this step is not required.
  1. In DRC Cloud Edition, navigate to the certificate management section.
  2. Upload the SSL client certificate exported from S/4HANA (Public Edition: Step 2.2; Private Edition: from STRUST in Step 2B Option A, Step 1).
  3. DRC uses this certificate to authenticate every inbound message from S/4HANA.

3.5 Configure GSP Communication Settings

This is done in the Manage Communication Settings – External Party app within DRC Cloud Edition. Configure settings for each India process you have activated. Each process requires two communication settings: one base setting for the authority channel, and one GSP-specific setting.

Reference: Process-Specific Settings

GST Return (India)

Step 1 — Base GSTN communication setting:

Field Value

Communication TypeGST Return (IN)
Identification NumberYour GSTIN number
AuthenticationOne-Time Password (OTP)
Additional PropertiesAdd service provider name (as maintained in S/4HANA) + Username registered in the GSTN Portal

Step 2 — GSP-specific communication setting:

For the second communication setting, select the communication type according to your GSP:

If using GSP Vayana:

Field Value

Communication TypeGSP Vayana for GST Returns (IN)
Identification NumberService provider name maintained in the business system
AuthenticationBasic
Additional AuthenticationKey Pair X.509 — upload the certificate generated per Vayana's documentation; enter the certificate password
Additional PropertiesAdd Client ID and/or Customer ID received from Vayana

If using GSP Excellon:

Field Value

Communication TypeGSP Excellon for GST Returns (IN)
Identification NumberService provider name maintained in the business system
AuthenticationBasic

E-Invoices (India)

E-invoice registration is processed via NIC (National Informatics Centre), not directly through GSTN. DRC routes these submissions through your chosen GSP to the NIC IRP (Invoice Registration Portal).

Step 1 — Base communication setting:

Field Value

Communication TypeE-Invoices (IN)
Identification NumberYour GSTIN number
AuthenticationBasic

Step 2 — GSP-specific communication setting:

If using GSP Vayana:

Field Value

Communication TypeGSP Vayana for E-Invoices (IN)
Identification NumberService provider name maintained in the business system
AuthenticationKey Pair X.509 — upload the certificate generated per Vayana's documentation; enter the certificate password
Additional PropertiesAdd Client ID and/or Customer ID received from Vayana

If using GSP Excellon:

Field Value

Communication TypeGSP Excellon for E-Invoices (IN)
Identification NumberService provider name maintained in the business system
AuthenticationBasic

E-Waybills (India)

E-waybill generation is also handled through NIC (National Informatics Centre) via the E-Waybill System. DRC routes these requests through your chosen GSP to NIC.

Step 1 — Base communication setting:

Field Value

Communication TypeE-Waybills (IN)
Identification NumberYour GSTIN number
AuthenticationBasic

Step 2 — GSP-specific communication setting:

If using GSP Vayana:

Field Value

Communication TypeGSP Vayana for E-Waybills (IN)
Identification NumberService provider name maintained in the business system
AuthenticationKey Pair X.509 — upload the certificate generated per Vayana's documentation; enter the certificate password
Additional PropertiesAdd Client ID and/or Customer ID received from Vayana

If using GSP Excellon:

Field Value

Communication TypeGSP Excellon for E-Waybills (IN)
Identification NumberService provider name maintained in the business system
AuthenticationBasic

Obtaining GSTINs for Sandbox Testing

Plan early — sandbox onboarding via your GSP can take two or more weeks.

The GSTN provides two environments: Sandbox (for testing) and Production. All testing should be done against the GSTN Sandbox before go-live.

Important: For GST Reporting, production GSTINs cannot be used in the GSTN Sandbox — authentication and report submission will not work. You must use the dedicated test GSTINs provided by your GSP. For E-Invoicing, production GSTINs may be accepted in the Sandbox environment. Guidelines might change, so check with your GSP for the latest sandbox rules before testing.

Contact your chosen GSP (Vayana or Excellon) and request sandbox/test environment access. The GSP provides:

  • Test GSTINs pre-registered in the GSTN Sandbox
  • Test credentials (API keys, certificates) for the sandbox environment

Once received:

  1. In DRC, configure the sandbox credentials and sandbox API endpoint in Manage Communication Settings – External Party (Step 3.5) instead of production values.
  2. In S/4HANA, assign the test GSTIN to a Business Place using SSCUI 101326 (Define Business Places for Goods and Services Tax for India).
Note: GSTN Sandbox OTP values for authentication are fixed test values — confirm the expected OTP with your GSP before testing.

Common Pitfalls

  • Configuring the service binding before finalizing the S/4HANA Communication Arrangement URL
  • Treating certificates as "optional until UAT" — mismatched certificates can surface as unexpected issues late in testing
  • Mismatched GSP/service-provider values across S/4HANA and DRC
  • Omitting the second (GSP-specific) communication setting in DRC — both the base GST Return (IN) setting and the GSP-specific setting are required
  • Starting GSTIN sandbox onboarding after technical setup is complete (lead times can exceed two weeks)
  • Creating more than one Communication Arrangement for the same scenario in S/4HANA

Pre-Go-Live Checklist

DRC Cloud Edition Setup (on SAP BTP)

  • Global Account has DRC Cloud Edition entitlement
  • Subaccount created in a supported region (India processes verified)
  • Correct service plan assigned: standard (production) or test (sandbox — routes to GSTN/NIC test environment)
  • DRC Cloud Edition application subscribed (plan matching subaccount purpose)
  • Service instance created (Default plan)
  • Service binding created (authentication type chosen, process = Other Processes)
  • Service binding URL noted
  • GST Return (India) process activated (if applicable)
  • E-Invoices (India) process activated (if applicable — routes to NIC IRP)
  • E-Waybills (India) process activated (if applicable — routes to NIC E-Waybill System)
  • User role collections assigned

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Scope items 5XU and 78L activated
  • GSTIN assigned to Business Place via SSCUI 101326
  • GSP Name maintained per Business Place / Company Code (Maintain Service Provider for eInvoice)
  • SSL client certificate exported
  • Communication System created (DRC endpoint URL)
  • Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0854 created and Integration Scenario field populated

S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • GSTIN assigned to Business Place via SSCUI 101326
  • GSP Name maintained per Business Place / Company Code (Maintain Service Provider for eInvoice)
  • If using X.509 (Option A): SSL client certificate imported/generated in STRUST; certificate uploaded to service binding (User Provided X.509); logical ports configured in SOA Management with X.509 authentication
  • If using OAuth 2.0 (Option B): Service binding created in BTP (Authentication Type: OAuth 2.0); OAuth 2.0 Client Profile created; OAuth 2.0 Client configured (OA2C_CONFIG) with service binding credentials; server root certificate from DRC imported into STRUST; RFC connection created and activated (SM59)

DRC Cloud Edition

  • Destination certificate generated/uploaded (DRC → S/4HANA)
  • Connectivity to S/4HANA configured (inbound service URL)
  • Business system mapped
  • SSL certificate from S/4HANA uploaded (X.509 authentication only)
  • (If GST Return) Base communication setting: GST Return (IN) with OTP auth + GSTIN + service provider + username
  • (If GST Return) GSP-specific setting: GSP Vayana for GST Returns (IN) or GSP Excellon for GST Returns (IN)
  • (If E-Invoices) Base communication setting: E-Invoices (IN) with GSTIN
  • (If E-Invoices) GSP-specific setting: GSP Vayana for E-Invoices (IN) or GSP Excellon for E-Invoices (IN)
  • (If E-Waybills) Base communication setting: E-Waybills (IN) with GSTIN
  • (If E-Waybills) GSP-specific setting: GSP Vayana for E-Waybills (IN) or GSP Excellon for E-Waybills (IN)

Testing

  • GSTN Sandbox access obtained via GSP
  • Authority response handling verified

References

entry.title: Crystal Reports and .Net Core
entry.content:
Are there any plans for Crystal Reports to be supported using .Net Core?

We are a 20+ year user of Crystal Reports and recently found out that once we go to .Net Core in our environment that the Crystal Reports Runtime will not work.  We are in the process of getting off of .Net Framework and I was surprised to find out that Crystal Reportds only support .Net Framework and not .Net Core and there is nothing published for a road map to indicate that it will support .Net Core.

Has anyone else tackled this issue and found a solution?

entry.title: Update on SAP Activate Road Map Viewer Accelerators for Central Finance
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I want to share an update on the accelerators available in the SAP Activate Road Map Viewer for Central Finance

Over the past few weeks, we have conducted a thorough review and cleanup of the accelerator content. This effort focused on resolving broken links, removing outdated references, and enhancing the overall reliability and usability of the materials. As a result, the accelerators are now more consistent and better aligned with current best practices.

This update aims to improve your experience when using SAP Activateas a guiding framework for your Central Finance implementations. By ensuring that all referenced materials are accessible and up to date, we support more efficient project execution and reduce friction during delivery

We encourage you to revisit the Central Finance content in the Road Map Viewer and take advantage of the improved accelerators. As always, your feedback is invaluable—please feel free to share your thoughts or suggestions to help us continue improving.

Best Regards,

Carla Nascimento

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud Advance Foreign Currency Valuation posting line items without trading partner
entry.content:
Hello. SAP Public Cloud Advance Foreign Currency Valuation keeps on posting line items without trading partner. We are trying to stop it by doing the setup and doing adjustment entries (should be zero). For this GL, it is not zero for Balance Transaction Currency and USD. How should we independently update/adjust these without touching the other currency columns? For USD, "Post Currency Adjustment" app seems to fix this but I have no idea how to adjust Balance Transaction Curr independently.

Or if you have other tips, please let me know! Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Deactivate an item in SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content:
Hi @asiawoods324 

I don't recall any configuration that could cause such error... Nor I'm aware of any defect on this.

I think the best approach is to raise a ticket to the LOD-SF-LMS component, by providing:

  1. The BizX Company ID (as per KBA 2655655);
  2. Support Access username (with access to Learning Administration), as per KBA 2088892;
  3. The affected item ID;
  4. If it's happening to specific items or to all of them;
  5. If the same happens in another tenant as well (such as Stage / Preview).

Also, I recommend enabling the setting reportStackTraceToUser (as per 2452616) and replicate the error, in order to get the full error log (which should be provided at the ticket as well).

Best regards,

entry.title: Project Systems How to Change a WBS element after it has been released.
entry.content: I created a project in 2024 and since then there has been spend and we are now placing the assets into service - up until April 2, 2026 everything was perfect and then someone changed the WBS element and removed a character and now the system will not allow the rest of the spend to be placed into service nor will it allow you to change it back to the original WBS ID.  Any help would be very much appreciated as we need to start depreciating the assets. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How can I create an action in RAP with mandatory para...

Hi @Andre_Fischer 

 This works, however the only effect is the red * in the UI. But the mandatory fields are not automatically checked.

Is this by design?

Sure we can build the validation logic in the behavior class, but in that case when the error message is displayed, the action popup is closed and they have to click again and enter all the fields again.

It makes for bad user experience.

entry.title: Clarification on STO and OBD Creation
entry.content:
Hi Team,

A concern was raised regarding STO creation with multiple line items for the same material, where delivery dates differ but the vendor remains the same.

Currently, when we run or attempt to create the OBD, the system generates separate OBDs for each line item based on the delivery dates. The question is whether it is possible to create a single OBD for all lines even if the delivery dates are different.

Please confirm if this is feasible, or share any alternative approach we can consider.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Clarification on STO and OBD Creation
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

I hope you are doing well.

After analyzing the reported behavior, we would like to clarify that the system is working according to the standard design of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. During the creation of outbound deliveries from Stock Transport Orders, the system evaluates predefined delivery split criteria, and one of the key factors considered is the delivery date (schedule line date). When STO line items contain different delivery dates, the system interprets them as requiring separate logistical processing and, therefore, creates multiple outbound deliveries, even if other data such as material or supplying plant remain the same.

According to KBA 3701562 (FAQ: Delivery split when creating deliveries – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition), delivery split is determined based on standard system criteria. Differences in delivery dates are one of the factors that can lead to delivery split. Similar behavior is also described in KBA 2961829 and KBA 3551006, although these are not specific to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, they reflect the same standard delivery split logic.

Please note that in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, delivery split logic is predefined and cannot be adjusted through configuration (SSCUI) or extensibility options. For this reason, when delivery dates differ, the system will always create separate outbound deliveries.

As a recommendation, if the business requirement is to have a single outbound delivery, it is necessary to ensure that all relevant split criteria are aligned, especially by maintaining the same delivery date across all STO line items.

Best regards,
Letícia

entry.title: SSO Migration from ADFS to Entra ID. IAS – Guidance Request
entry.content:
Hello,

We are planning to migrate our corporate Identity Provider from ADFS to Microsoft Entra ID for SSO authentication. SuccessFactors (including LMS), using IAS. At this stage, we are in the planning phase and would like to begin with the LMS Preview environment before proceeding to Production. 

System / Landscape:

  • Product: SAP SuccessFactors (Learning)
  • Identity Layer: SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS)
  • Current Corporate IdP: ADFS
  • Planning Corporate IdP: Microsoft Entra ID
  • Environments: LMS Preview (Test) and LMS Production

We would appreciate guidance on the recommended approach to configure Entra ID as a new Corporate IdP in IAS alongside the existing ADFS.

Thank you,

Serguei,

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports and .Net Core
entry.content:
The short answer, afaik, is no.
If you search this forum, you'll find the same question has been answered multiple times.
Bottom line: stay on .NET 4.81 or lower (or delegate from your .NET Core to a .NET 4.81 just for the Crystal aspect).
